items.definition_en: A terminal is a building at an airport or station where people start or end their journey.
items.example_en: We waited for our friends at the terminal.

items.definition_en: A runway is a long road at an airport where airplanes take off and land.
items.example_en: The plane is getting ready on the runway.

items.definition_en: A check-in desk is where you get your boarding pass and drop off your bags.
items.example_en: We lined up at the check-in desk early.

items.definition_en: A departure gate is the place where you wait to get on your plane.
items.example_en: Our departure gate changed at the last minute.

items.definition_en: A waiting room is a place where people sit until their train or plane is ready.
items.example_en: We stayed in the waiting room before boarding.

items.definition_en: An information desk is where you can ask for help or find out information.
items.example_en: She asked for directions at the information desk.

items.definition_en: A ticket office is a place where you buy tickets at the station or airport.
items.example_en: He went to the ticket office to buy a train ticket.

items.definition_en: Baggage claim is where you pick up your bags after a flight.
items.example_en: We went to baggage claim to get our suitcases.

items.definition_en: A platform is a place beside the tracks where people get on or off a train.
items.example_en: Our train arrived at platform three.

items.definition_en: A lounge is a comfortable room where you can relax before your flight.
items.example_en: We waited in the lounge until boarding started.

items.definition_en: A security check is a place where your bags and you are checked for safety.
items.example_en: The security check took a very long time.

items.definition_en: Customs is the place where your bags are checked for goods when you enter a country.
items.example_en: We showed our bags at customs after we landed.

items.definition_en: A car park is a place where you leave your car at the airport or station.
items.example_en: We parked our car in the car park next to the station.

items.definition_en: An escalator is moving stairs that carry people up or down between floors.
items.example_en: We used the escalator to reach the gates.

items.definition_en: A locker is a small cupboard you can lock to keep your things safe.
items.example_en: He put his bag in a locker at the station.

items.definition_en: An announcement is an official message heard at the airport or train station.
items.example_en: We listened for our flight in the announcement.

items.definition_en: A station hall is a large open space where passengers wait or move between areas.
items.example_en: The station hall was crowded at rush hour.

items.definition_en: A turnstile is a gate with metal bars that turns and lets one person pass at a time.
items.example_en: You need your ticket to use the turnstile.

items.definition_en: A boarding pass is a card that shows your seat and lets you get on the plane.
items.example_en: The staff checked my boarding pass at the gate.

items.definition_en: A waiting area is a place in the station or airport where you sit before leaving.
items.example_en: We found seats in the waiting area before the train arrived.
